entity.fiscal_year_end_convention: Friday nearest June 30; FY2026 is a 53-week year ending 2026-07-03

scope.intended_use: Fundamental public-equity research and scenario valuation; not investment advice

reporting_basis.supplemental: Company non-GAAP metrics retained only as separately labeled context
reporting_basis.forecast_convention: FY2026E equals nine-month actual results plus the midpoint of SanDisk's FY2026 Q4 guidance; FY2027E-FY2030E are explicit Bear/Base/Bull assumptions
reporting_basis.historical_perimeter: FY2023-FY2025 includes carve-out periods before the 2025-02-21 separation from Western Digital and therefore contains allocated corporate and shared-service costs that may not represent standalone economics
reporting_basis.segment_definition: One legal operating segment; Cloud was renamed Datacenter and Client was renamed Edge in FY2026

business_archetype.modeling_implication: Do not capitalize peak-cycle gross margins indefinitely; show transition and normalized earnings separately

tier_definitions.E0: audited filing, filed quarterly statement, or directly observed market datum
tier_definitions.E1: official company earnings release, investor material, or operating disclosure
tier_definitions.E2: traceable partner, customer, or supplier disclosure
tier_definitions.E3: credible third-party market research or forecast

evidence_policy.load_bearing_rule: Prefer E0/E1 evidence; E3 evidence may frame industry scenarios but cannot be presented as SanDisk-reported fact
evidence_policy.fact_assumption_separation: Every model assumption is explicitly registered and distinct from sourced actuals
evidence_policy.cutoff_rule: Exclude facts first published after the information cutoff even when they concern an earlier fiscal period
evidence_policy.conflict_rule: Preserve conflicting evidence and disclose differences rather than silently averaging

current_state.enterprise_ssd: BiCS10 sampling disclosed; do not assume full production economics without later qualification and ramp evidence
current_state.capital_return: $6.0 billion repurchase authorization disclosed; execution not evidenced by the cutoff

limitations: The NASDAQ price page is dynamic and the captured price is an intraday observation, not an archival close.
FY2026 Q4 and full-year results were not published by the cutoff; Q4 values are guidance midpoints, not actuals.
Industry forecasts from Kioxia, Micron, and TrendForce are corroborating scenario evidence and are not SanDisk guidance.
The model does not treat the $6.0 billion repurchase authorization as executed.
The $972 million Nanya investment paid after the FY2026 Q3 balance-sheet date is reflected only in pro-forma excess cash.
